citation.article_title: Simulating next-generation sequencing datasets from empirical mutation and sequencing models
citation.author: Stephens
citation.unstructured_citation: Stephens, Z. D., Hudson, M. E., Mainzer, L. S., Taschuk, M., Weber, M. R., & Iyer, R. K. (2016). Simulating next-generation sequencing datasets from empirical mutation and sequencing models. PLOS ONE, 11(11), e0167047. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0167047

citation.article_title: Summarizing and correcting the GC content bias in high-throughput sequencing
citation.author: Benjamini
citation.unstructured_citation: Benjamini, Y., & Speed, T. P. (2012). Summarizing and correcting the GC content bias in high-throughput sequencing. Nucleic Acids Research, 40(10), e72. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gks001

citation.article_title: Characterizing and measuring bias in sequence data
citation.author: Ross
citation.unstructured_citation: Ross, M. G., Russ, C., Costello, M., Hollinger, A., Lennon, N. J., Hegarty, R., Nusbaum, C., & Jaffe, D. B. (2013). Characterizing and measuring bias in sequence data. Genome Biology, 14(5), R51. https://doi.org/10.1186/gb-2013-14-5-r51

citation.article_title: A comparison of tools for the simulation of genomic next-generation sequencing data
citation.author: Escalona
citation.unstructured_citation: Escalona, M., Rocha, S., & Posada, D. (2016). A comparison of tools for the simulation of genomic next-generation sequencing data. Nature Reviews Genetics, 17(8), 459–469. https://doi.org/10.1038/nrg.2016.57

citation.article_title: Systematic review of next-generation sequencing simulators: Computational tools, features and perspectives
citation.author: Zhao
citation.unstructured_citation: Zhao, M., Liu, D., & Qu, H. (2017). Systematic review of next-generation sequencing simulators: Computational tools, features and perspectives. Briefings in Functional Genomics, 16(3), 121–128. https://doi.org/10.1093/bfgp/elw012

citation.article_title: A broad survey of DNA sequence data simulation tools
citation.author: Alosaimi

D., Allali, I., Mazandu, G. K., Mulder, N. J., & Chimusa, E. R. (2020). A broad survey of DNA sequence data simulation tools. Briefings in Functional Genomics, 19(1), 49–59. https://doi.org/10.1093/bfgp/elz033

citation.author: Milhaven

Frontiers in Genetics, 10, 736. https://doi.org/10.3389/fgene.2019.00736

(2021). Methods and feasibility study for exome sequencing as a universal second-tier test in newborn screening. Genetics in Medicine, 23(4), 767–776. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41436-020-01058-w

citation.author: Shah

citation.article_title: Needlestack: An ultra-sensitive variant caller for multi-sample next generation sequencing data
citation.author: Delhomme

G., Alcala, N., Leblay, N., Voegele, C., Vallée, M., Chopard, P., Chabrier, A., Abedi-Ardekani, B., Gaborieau, V., Holcatova, I., Janout, V., Foretová, L., Milosavljevic, S., Zaridze, D., Mukeriya, A., Brambilla, E., Brennan, P., … Foll, M. (2020). Needlestack: An ultra-sensitive variant caller for multi-sample next generation sequencing data. NAR Genomics and Bioinformatics, 2(2), lqaa021. https://doi.org/10.1093/nargab/lqaa021
